rationale: An LLM capped at floor-level capabilities (trivial API DoS via oversized payload, basic Python scripting) provides minimal uplift for this OC3 ransomware scenario, where the binding technical constraints — EDR/SOC evasion, sophisticated lateral movement, privilege escalation, LotL techniques — all lie far above this capability ceiling. The LLM might modestly assist with reconnaissance scripting and basic vulnerability enumeration, but these are not the limiting factors that prevent actors below OC3 from executing big-game ransomware campaigns. Non-technical barriers (operational budget, team, ransomware affiliate infrastructure, risk tolerance) remain entirely unaffected. The resulting estimate of 3-5 represents near-baseline actor counts with a very modest 1.0-1.7x multiplier, reflecting that this specific LLM capability level provides negligible barrier reduction for this sophisticated attack scenario.
